title: Papillary Thyroid Cancer: Genetic Alterations and Molecular Biomarker Investigations.
author_string: Abdullah, Mardiaty Iryani, Junit, Sarni Mat, Ng, Khoon Leong, Jayapalan, Jaime Jacqueline, Karikalan, Barani et al.
abstract: Papillary thyroid cancer (PTC) is the most prevalent form of malignancy among all cancers of the thyroid. It is also one of the few cancers with a rapidly increasing incidence. PTC is usually contained within the thyroid gland and generally biologically indolent. Prognosis of the cancer is excellent, with less than 2% mortality at 5 years. However, more than 25% of patients with PTC developed a recurrence during a long term follow-up. The present article provides an updated condensed overview of PTC, which focuses mainly on the molecular alterations involved and recent biomarker investigations.
